reasoning: The World Health Organization rarely declares new pandemics (approx. 2 since 2000, ~8% base annual rate). The knowledge cutoff date is October 26, 2025, leaving only about 65 days remaining in the resolution period. Applying the annual base rate to the remaining fraction of the year yields roughly a 1.4% probability. Without any specific recent evidence of an imminent and widely-acknowledged outbreak demanding WHO's most severe global classification, the probability remains near its logical baseline of ~1%. I am submitting 0.03 as a conservative extreme lower bound estimation.
